src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: successful user registration and login via firebase module: authentication source files or pages: 1 covered methods: preconditions: - firebase authentication is enabled. - user does not exist in the system. test data: - valid nickname: `john_doe` - valid password: `securepass123!` - valid email: `john@example.com` test steps: 1. user enters `john_doe` as nickname, `securepass123!` as password, and `john@example.com` as email. 2. system validates input (nickname/password/email format). 3. user clicks "register." 4. system creates user in firebase authentication. 5. user logs in with the same credentials. expected result: - user account is created successfully. - authentication token is generated and stored. - user is redirected to the home screen with valid session. unit: project:

src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-chat-002 title: verify many-to-many user-chat relationships module: chat associations source files or pages: 2 covered methods: preconditions: - user1, user2, and user3 exist. - chat1 exists between user1 and user2. - chat2 exists between user1 and user3. test data: - chat1: `{ "id": 1, "user1": user1, "user2": user2 }` - chat2: `{ "id": 2, "user1": user1, "user2": user3 }` test steps: 1. user1 sends a message to user2 via chat1. 2. user1 sends a message to user3 via chat2. 3. verify chat1 only shows messages between user1 and user2. 4. verify chat2 only shows messages between user1 and user3. expected result: - messages are correctly segmented by chat. - no cross-chat message leakage. unit: project:
